hotel.name: Rimklong Boutique Hotel
hotel.description: One of Trat Town's first stylish small hotels, set in a renovated brick and wood shophouse in the old town area. The building's character shines through with exposed brick walls and wooden details. Central location near the river, night market, and local restaurants. A step up from basic transit hotels without luxury prices.
highlights: Central old town location near night market
Stylish rooms with air conditioning and hot water

similarHotels.name: Trat 101 Hotel
similarHotels.description: A boutique hotel in the heart of Trat's old town on Thana Charoen Road, decorated with oriental vintage furniture and featuring original 100+ year-old wooden floors. Walking distance to the nightly food market, historical sites, and small restaurants. The perfect base for exploring Trat Town before heading to the islands. Rooms are clean and atmospheric with air conditioning.
highlights: Located in historic old town with original wooden floors
Walking distance to Trat Night Market and ferry pier transport
Vintage boutique atmosphere at budget prices

similarHotels.name: Oasis Koh Chang
similarHotels.description: Budget bungalows set on a hillside above Lonely Beach with a peaceful jungle atmosphere and swimming pool. Choice of fan bungalows (cheapest) or air-conditioned rooms. The restaurant serves good Thai food. Lonely Beach is Koh Chang's backpacker hub with beach bars and the island's best nightlife just a short walk downhill.
highlights: Swimming pool — rare at budget prices on Koh Chang
Jungle hillside setting with peaceful atmosphere
Walking distance to Lonely Beach bars and restaurants

aiContent.hookIntro: Booking Rimklong Boutique Hotel without knowing its shophouse location near the busy night market can lead to unexpected street noise disrupting your sleep.
aiContent.intro: Rimklong Boutique Hotel offers a stylish stay in a renovated historic shophouse with exposed brick and wooden details, providing air-conditioned rooms with hot water at budget prices. It's ideal for couples and culture seekers exploring Trat Town's old area without paying for luxury.
whyBook: Renovated shophouse exposes charming brick walls and wood accents.
Central spot walks to Trat night market and river views.
Stylish rooms include air conditioning and reliable hot water.
Elevates beyond basic transit hotels at affordable rates.
whyNotBook: No on-site pool or spa for relaxation downtime.
Central old town spot picks up night market noise evenings.
Small hotel size limits room variety and amenities.
Basic setup lacks luxury extras like room service.
Proximity to river may bring occasional dampness in rainy season.
aiContent.skipIf: Skip if you're traveling with kids needing pool access or seeking quiet beach vibes away from town bustle.
aiContent.detailedReview: Checking into Rimklong Boutique Hotel feels like stepping into Trat Town's preserved history, with the front desk in the ground floor of a renovated brick and wood shophouse welcoming guests amid exposed walls and wooden beams. The vibe is intimate and characterful, one of the area's first small stylish hotels, appealing to those wanting a cultural immersion without high costs. Rooms deliver on style with air conditioning keeping things cool and hot water showers providing comfort after exploring, though spaces are compact fitting the boutique scale. Standout is the building's authentic old town charm, blending seamlessly with the surroundings. Food isn't on-site, but service is friendly and efficient, typical for a small operation focused on transit stays upgraded from basics. Guests here are often couples wandering the night market or culture seekers hopping ferries to islands, creating a relaxed, low-key profile. Overall, it shines for short stays emphasizing location over extensive facilities, offering a genuine slice of Trat's heritage at 1,000-1,500 THB per night.
aiContent.locationReality: Rimklong Boutique Hotel sits in Trat Town's old town, a short walk to the river and night market, making it easy to stroll to local eateries in under 5 minutes. The nearest main attraction, like the ferry piers for Koh Chang, requires a 10-15 minute Grab ride costing around 100-150 THB. Beach access demands transport to Koh Chang, about 30-45 minutes by ferry from Laem Ngop pier, 20 minutes away by taxi.
localSignals: Walking distance to Trat night market in old town area.
Near Trat River, under 5 minutes on foot.
Rooms equipped with air conditioning and hot water.
No on-site pool or restaurant; amenities limited to basics.
Central location near local restaurants and likely 7-Eleven within 5-minute walk.
aiContent.travellerNote: Guests often praise the historic shophouse character and central location for easy access to markets but note the potential for street noise from nearby bustle and lack of on-site dining options.
bookingTips: Request rooms facing away from the street to minimize night market sounds.
Check Agoda for deals, often cheaper than Booking.com in Thailand.
Prices spike in November-February high season for island ferries.
Verify hot water consistency before booking, as it's a key room feature.
Confirm no construction nearby, given old town renovations common.

similarHotels.howDifferent: Cheaper with oriental vintage decor on Thana Charoen Road, lacking Rimklong's brick shophouse exposure.

similarHotels.name: Oasis Koh Chang (500-1,200 THB/night (~$14-34 USD)) — Budget bungalows set on a hillside above Lonely Beach with a peaceful jungle atmosphere and swimming pool. Choice of fan bungalows (cheapest
similarHotels.howDifferent: Offers bungalows with pool on Koh Chang's Lonely Beach, contrasting Rimklong's mainland town shophouse without beach access.

faq.q: Is Rimklong Boutique Hotel suitable for couples?
faq.a: Yes, its stylish shophouse rooms and central old town location make it ideal for couples seeking cultural vibes near the night market.

faq.q: What amenities are in Rimklong Boutique Hotel rooms?
faq.a: Rooms feature air conditioning and hot water, with exposed brick and wooden details for a characterful stay.

faq.q: How far is Rimklong Boutique Hotel from Koh Chang ferries?
faq.a: It's in Trat Town, about 20 minutes by taxi to Laem Ngop pier for ferries to Koh Chang.

faq.q: Does Rimklong Boutique Hotel have a pool?
faq.a: No, it lacks a pool, focusing instead on its historic building and location perks.

faq.q: Is breakfast included at Rimklong Boutique Hotel?
faq.a: Breakfast is not mentioned as included; nearby local restaurants provide options within walking distance.

faq.q: What's the vibe at Rimklong Boutique Hotel?
faq.a: The vibe is intimate and historic, in a renovated shophouse blending old town charm with modern comforts like AC.

aiContent.urgencyLine: Rooms at Rimklong Boutique Hotel with shophouse character book out 1-2 months ahead for December-February peak ferry season — check today.
aiContent.methodology: We rated Rimklong Boutique Hotel based on its location, room features, and value from provided data. We weighted central old town access highest because Trat serves as a key transit hub to islands. Prices and availability change — always verify before booking. Last updated: 2026-04.
aiContent.metaDescription: Rimklong Boutique Hotel in Trat Town delivers historic shophouse charm with AC rooms near night market, but expect street noise and no pool. Budget stay for couples; book now for culture trips. (148 characters)
